source_registry.short_title: VLT® AQUA Drive FC 202 Selection Guide 0.25 kW – 1.4 MW
source_registry.scope_summary: Product overview, installation features, efficiency, harmonic mitigation, cascade control, and references to ordering typecodes and technical data sections
notes_relevant_to_decoding_or_datasheet_: Ordering typecode sections referenced on pages 49, 58, 64
Multiple enclosure sizes A–F and enclosed drives referenced
Options structure includes A options (fieldbus), B options (functional), C options (cascade/relay), D option (24 V backup)

typecode_structure.explanation: Ordering typecode exists and is defined in later sections of the selection guide

typecode_values.meaning: Drive power range
Supported supply voltage range
Supported supply voltage range
Supported supply voltage range
Supported supply voltage range

typecode_values.code: A, B, C, D, E, F
typecode_values.meaning: Enclosure sizes
typecode_values.notes: Multiple enclosure size groups referenced

typecode_values.meaning: Ingress protection range
Communication options
Additional functional modules
Cascade control related options
Backup supply option
Setup wizard activated at first power up or after factory reset
typecode_values.notes: Guides through motor setup and water/pump feature selection

typecode_values.meaning: Measures motor parameters and optimizes drive settings at standstill without disconnecting the load
typecode_values.notes: Also referenced as AMA and accessible through SmartStart

typecode_values.meaning: Adapts voltage to the current load situation to reduce energy consumption
typecode_values.notes: Also described as automatic adaption to application

typecode_values.meaning: Measures system characteristics and automatically finds process controller settings for stable and precise system control
typecode_values.notes: Described as easy commissioning and super-easy commissioning

typecode_values.meaning: Drive adapts the set-point according to the flow
Removes clogs from impellers by reversing the direction of the flow in cycles
typecode_values.notes: Can be used as proactive measure to avoid damaging the pump; also described as standard and as replacement for external deragger relay

typecode_values.meaning: Helps to avoid water hammering by filling pipes smoothly
Protects the pump from damage; if a set-point is not reached, the drive assumes that the pipe is dry or there is a leakage
Saves energy by stopping the pump when there is no demand
Dedicated startup and stop ramps for specific applications
Two motors share one drive
typecode_values.notes: Also described as suitable for applications with 2 motors or 2 pumps sharing 1 drive

typecode_values.meaning: Speed control a single pump in a multi pump system
typecode_values.notes: Described as cost attractive solution in for example booster sets

typecode_values.meaning: Control up to 8 drives and pumps to ensure smooth operation of the overall pump system
typecode_values.notes: Use Master/Follower mode to reduce energy consumption compared to traditional technology across the line for on/off cycling of pumps or blowers, and valve throttling

typecode_values.meaning: Integrated cascade controller based on Modbus RTU communication protocol
typecode_values.notes: Ready to use; simply activate it with a software license key

typecode_values.meaning: Automatic drive detection supports drive maintenance without downtime by re-allocating roles in the master/follower architecture
typecode_values.notes: After maintenance is complete, the cascade controller automatically incorporates the drive back into the loop and allows re-allocation of master/follower roles

typecode_values.meaning: Cloud-free artificial intelligence embedded in the drive automatically defines operating baseline parameters corresponding to specific applications, at all speeds and real-life operating cycles
typecode_values.notes: Reduced installation time and predictive maintenance lower operating costs and boost uptime

typecode_values.meaning: Cooling concept directing air through a rear cooling channel so up to 90% of the drive’s heat loss is removed directly outside the installation room
typecode_values.notes: Described as reducing investment for air cooling systems by up to 90%

typecode_values.meaning: Accessory mounting kit for small and mid-range drives enables heat losses to be directed directly outside the panel room and into designated air ducts
Standard cable capability without need for additional components
typecode_values.notes: Presented as installation cost reduction feature

typecode_values.code: 3C3 PCB coating as standard on 90 kW and larger drives
typecode_values.meaning: Printed circuit board protection standard for larger drives
typecode_values.notes: Guide also states VLT® AQUA Drive conforms to class 3C2 (IEC 60721-3-3) as standard and special coating can comply with class 3C3; all drives above 90 kW delivered with 3C3 coating as standard

typecode_values.meaning: Version intended to ensure components remain firmly in place in pump environments characterized by high vibration levels
Operating ambient temperature range without derating
typecode_values.notes: One statement says VLT® AQUA Drives up to 315 kW operate in ambient temperatures from -25 to 50 °C without derating; another states VLT® AQUA Drive can operate in ambient temperatures between -25 and +50 °C, without derating

typecode_values.meaning: Certified harmonic mitigation solution
typecode_values.notes: Distortion levels of no more than 5% THDi and power factor of the system re-established at 1

typecode_values.meaning: Certified harmonic mitigation solution
typecode_values.notes: Specially designed to be connected in front of a VLT® drive; harmonic current distortion generated back to the mains is reduced to a minimum

typecode_values.meaning: Certified harmonic mitigation solution
typecode_values.notes: Provide total harmonic current distortion (THDi) of maximum 5%

typecode_values.meaning: Certified harmonic mitigation solution
typecode_values.notes: Robust and cost-effective harmonic solution for higher power range above 250 kW

typecode_values.meaning: Certified harmonic mitigation solution
typecode_values.notes: Supply current distortion (THDi) about 3-4%

technical_tables.table_name: Flow vs Power Relationship for Centrifugal Pumps
technical_tables.applies_to: Centrifugal pumps and blowers in water supply systems
notes: 20% reduction in pump speed/flowrate can result in up to 50% energy reduction
Energy consumption for pumps typically represents 60-80% of total energy consumption in water supply systems
conditions: Applies to systems characterized by friction loss
Applies to centrifugal or rotodynamic pumps

technical_tables.table_name: Flow vs Input Power for Wastewater Blowers/Pumps
technical_tables.applies_to: Positive displacement blowers or pumps in wastewater treatment
notes: A 30% reduction in speed will result in 30% energy savings assuming constant pressure
Blowers or surface aerators typically consume 40-70% of total energy in wastewater treatment plants
conditions: Applies to positive displacement blowers or pumps

technical_tables.table_name: Energy Consumption Comparison of Cascade Control Methods
notes: Master/Follower mode significantly reduces energy consumption compared to traditional constant speed systems
conditions: Comparison based on example system shown in guide

rows: NOT FOUND IN THE UPLOADED DOCUMENTS
notes: Digital Cascade Controller based on Modbus RTU communication protocol

rows: Central solution; operates only when needed
notes: Central AAF solution can support up to 50 drives
Harmonic noise kept under 3% on all AC drives in system with central AAF solution
conditions: Performance depends on grid quality, imbalance, and pre-distortion

rows: Cost savings compared to traditional AC drives
notes: Back-channel cooling significantly reduces cooling system size

mapping_rules.mapping_logic_from_documents: Select rated power within the overall supported range 0.25 kW – 1.4 MW
Select supply voltage based on supported ranges: 200-240 V, 380-480 V, 525-600 V, 525-690 V
Map enclosure code to one of the enclosure sizes A, B, C, D, E, F
Map selected IP rating within range IP00 to IP66
Map option groups A, B, C, D to fieldbus, functional extensions, cascade/relay, and 24 V backup respectively

constraints.exact_rule: VLT® AQUA Drives up to 315 kW operate in ambient temperatures from -25 to 50 °C without derating
VLT® AQUA Drive can operate in ambient temperatures between -25 and +50 °C without derating
3C3 PCB coating is standard on 90 kW and larger drives
VLT® AQUA Drive conforms to class 3C2 (IEC 60721-3-3) as standard; special coating can comply with class 3C3
Harmonic mitigation designed to meet IEEE-519 2014 Guide requirements for voltage THD limits at the Point of Common Coupling
Central advanced active filter solution ensures harmonic noise is kept under 3% on all AC drives in the system
Low harmonic drives provide total harmonic current distortion (THDi) of maximum 5%
Active front-end systems provide supply current distortion (THDi) of about 3-4%
12-pulse drives are used for higher power range applications above 250 kW
Digital Cascade Controller is license-based and activated via software license key
Digital Cascade Controller enables configuration of up to eight pumps in multiple-pump systems
Back-channel cooling transfers up to 90% of heat away from the room
Installation cost savings of up to 15% compared to traditional AC drives
